fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Fixing x:B, we must show that \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}gx\approx _{\mathcal {A}}gy is a proposition. But this is a retract of \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}gx\approx _{\mathcal {A}}y, which is a proposition by the univalence of \mathcal {A}; thus the result follows from the closure of propositions under retracts ().

fr:title.text: Restriction of path objects along quasi-inverses

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Restriction of path objects along quasi-inverses

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {A} be a path object and let B be a type. Given a quasi-inverse function g\colon B\to \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} , we can restrict the path object \mathcal {A} to a path object structure g^*\mathcal {A} on B as follows:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} \mathopen {}\left \lvert g^*\mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} &:\equiv B\\ x \approx _{g^*\mathcal {A}} y &:\equiv gx \approx _{\mathcal {A}} gy\\ \mathsf {rx}_{g^*\mathcal {A}}x &:\equiv \mathsf {rx}_{\mathcal {A}} \mathopen {}\left \lparen gx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Fixing x:B, we must show that \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:B\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}gx\approx _{\mathcal {A}}gy is a proposition. But this is a retract of \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}gx\approx _{\mathcal {A}}y, which is a proposition by the univalence of \mathcal {A}; thus the result follows from the closure of propositions under retracts ().
